sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 8.68 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.8)

statement.latex: Let$G= < a,b r=1 >$where$r$is a cyclically reduced word that is not a proper power of any word in$a,b$. If$G$is residually finite, is$G_t= <a,b r^t=1 >$,$t > 1$, residually finite? Editors' comment:\/ An analogous fact was proved for relative one-relator groups (S.\,J.\,Pride, Proc. Amer. Math. Soc., 136, no.\,2 (2008), 377--386). { C.\,Y.\,Tang} Yes, it is (D.\,T.\,Wise, The structure of groups w...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
